''' Constants and classes for matlab 5 read and write

See also mio5_utils.pyx where these same constants arise as c enums.

If you make changes in this file, don't forget to change mio5_utils.pyx
'''
import numpy as np

from ._miobase import convert_dtypes


__all__ = [
    'MDTYPES', 'MatlabFunction', 'MatlabObject', 'MatlabOpaque',
    'NP_TO_MTYPES', 'NP_TO_MXTYPES', 'OPAQUE_DTYPE', 'codecs_template',
    'mat_struct', 'mclass_dtypes_template', 'mclass_info', 'mdtypes_template',
    'miCOMPRESSED', 'miDOUBLE', 'miINT16', 'miINT32', 'miINT64', 'miINT8',
    'miMATRIX', 'miSINGLE', 'miUINT16', 'miUINT32', 'miUINT64', 'miUINT8',
    'miUTF16', 'miUTF32', 'miUTF8', 'mxCELL_CLASS', 'mxCHAR_CLASS',
    'mxDOUBLE_CLASS', 'mxFUNCTION_CLASS', 'mxINT16_CLASS', 'mxINT32_CLASS',
    'mxINT64_CLASS', 'mxINT8_CLASS', 'mxOBJECT_CLASS',
    'mxOBJECT_CLASS_FROM_MATRIX_H', 'mxOPAQUE_CLASS', 'mxSINGLE_CLASS',
    'mxSPARSE_CLASS', 'mxSTRUCT_CLASS', 'mxUINT16_CLASS', 'mxUINT32_CLASS',
    'mxUINT64_CLASS', 'mxUINT8_CLASS'
]
miINT8 = 1
miUINT8 = 2
miINT16 = 3
miUINT16 = 4
miINT32 = 5
miUINT32 = 6
miSINGLE = 7
miDOUBLE = 9
miINT64 = 12
miUINT64 = 13
miMATRIX = 14
miCOMPRESSED = 15
miUTF8 = 16
miUTF16 = 17
miUTF32 = 18

mxCELL_CLASS = 1
mxSTRUCT_CLASS = 2
# The March 2008 edition of "Matlab 7 MAT-File Format" says that
# mxOBJECT_CLASS = 3, whereas matrix.h says that mxLOGICAL = 3.
# Matlab 2008a appears to save logicals as type 9, so we assume that
# the document is correct. See type 18, below.
mxOBJECT_CLASS = 3
mxCHAR_CLASS = 4
mxSPARSE_CLASS = 5
mxDOUBLE_CLASS = 6
mxSINGLE_CLASS = 7
mxINT8_CLASS = 8
mxUINT8_CLASS = 9
mxINT16_CLASS = 10
mxUINT16_CLASS = 11
mxINT32_CLASS = 12
mxUINT32_CLASS = 13
# The following are not in the March 2008 edition of "Matlab 7
# MAT-File Format," but were guessed from matrix.h.
mxINT64_CLASS = 14
mxUINT64_CLASS = 15
mxFUNCTION_CLASS = 16
# Not doing anything with these at the moment.
mxOPAQUE_CLASS = 17  # This appears to be a function workspace
# Thread 'saving/loading symbol table of annymous functions',
# octave-maintainers, April-May 2007
# https://lists.gnu.org/archive/html/octave-maintainers/2007-04/msg00031.html
# https://lists.gnu.org/archive/html/octave-maintainers/2007-05/msg00032.html
# (Was/Deprecated: https://www-old.cae.wisc.edu/pipermail/octave-maintainers/2007-May/002824.html)
mxOBJECT_CLASS_FROM_MATRIX_H = 18

mdtypes_template = {
    miINT8: 'i1',
    miUINT8: 'u1',
    miINT16: 'i2',
    miUINT16: 'u2',
    miINT32: 'i4',
    miUINT32: 'u4',
    miSINGLE: 'f4',
    miDOUBLE: 'f8',
    miINT64: 'i8',
    miUINT64: 'u8',
    miUTF8: 'u1',
    miUTF16: 'u2',
    miUTF32: 'u4',
    'file_header': [('description', 'S116'),
                    ('subsystem_offset', 'i8'),
                    ('version', 'u2'),
                    ('endian_test', 'S2')],
    'tag_full': [('mdtype', 'u4'), ('byte_count', 'u4')],
    'tag_smalldata':[('byte_count_mdtype', 'u4'), ('data', 'S4')],
    'array_flags': [('data_type', 'u4'),
                    ('byte_count', 'u4'),
                    ('flags_class','u4'),
                    ('nzmax', 'u4')],
    'U1': 'U1',
    }

mclass_dtypes_template = {
    mxINT8_CLASS: 'i1',
    mxUINT8_CLASS: 'u1',
    mxINT16_CLASS: 'i2',
    mxUINT16_CLASS: 'u2',
    mxINT32_CLASS: 'i4',
    mxUINT32_CLASS: 'u4',
    mxINT64_CLASS: 'i8',
    mxUINT64_CLASS: 'u8',
    mxSINGLE_CLASS: 'f4',
    mxDOUBLE_CLASS: 'f8',
    }

mclass_info = {
    mxINT8_CLASS: 'int8',
    mxUINT8_CLASS: 'uint8',
    mxINT16_CLASS: 'int16',
    mxUINT16_CLASS: 'uint16',
    mxINT32_CLASS: 'int32',
    mxUINT32_CLASS: 'uint32',
    mxINT64_CLASS: 'int64',
    mxUINT64_CLASS: 'uint64',
    mxSINGLE_CLASS: 'single',
    mxDOUBLE_CLASS: 'double',
    mxCELL_CLASS: 'cell',
    mxSTRUCT_CLASS: 'struct',
    mxOBJECT_CLASS: 'object',
    mxCHAR_CLASS: 'char',
    mxSPARSE_CLASS: 'sparse',
    mxFUNCTION_CLASS: 'function',
    mxOPAQUE_CLASS: 'opaque',
    }

NP_TO_MTYPES = {
    'f8': miDOUBLE,
    'c32': miDOUBLE,
    'c24': miDOUBLE,
    'c16': miDOUBLE,
    'f4': miSINGLE,
    'c8': miSINGLE,
    'i8': miINT64,
    'i4': miINT32,
    'i2': miINT16,
    'i1': miINT8,
    'u8': miUINT64,
    'u4': miUINT32,
    'u2': miUINT16,
    'u1': miUINT8,
    'S1': miUINT8,
    'U1': miUTF16,
    'b1': miUINT8,  # not standard but seems MATLAB uses this (gh-4022)
    }


NP_TO_MXTYPES = {
    'f8': mxDOUBLE_CLASS,
    'c32': mxDOUBLE_CLASS,
    'c24': mxDOUBLE_CLASS,
    'c16': mxDOUBLE_CLASS,
    'f4': mxSINGLE_CLASS,
    'c8': mxSINGLE_CLASS,
    'i8': mxINT64_CLASS,
    'i4': mxINT32_CLASS,
    'i2': mxINT16_CLASS,
    'i1': mxINT8_CLASS,
    'u8': mxUINT64_CLASS,
    'u4': mxUINT32_CLASS,
    'u2': mxUINT16_CLASS,
    'u1': mxUINT8_CLASS,
    'S1': mxUINT8_CLASS,
    'b1': mxUINT8_CLASS,  # not standard but seems MATLAB uses this
    }

''' Before release v7.1 (release 14) matlab (TM) used the system
default character encoding scheme padded out to 16-bits. Release 14
and later use Unicode. When saving character data, R14 checks if it
can be encoded in 7-bit ascii, and saves in that format if so.'''

codecs_template = {
    miUTF8: {'codec': 'utf_8', 'width': 1},
    miUTF16: {'codec': 'utf_16', 'width': 2},
    miUTF32: {'codec': 'utf_32','width': 4},
    }


def _convert_codecs(template, byte_order):
    ''' Convert codec template mapping to byte order

    Set codecs not on this system to None

    Parameters
    ----------
    template : mapping
       key, value are respectively codec name, and root name for codec
       (without byte order suffix)
    byte_order : {'<', '>'}
       code for little or big endian

    Returns
    -------
    codecs : dict
       key, value are name, codec (as in .encode(codec))
    '''
    codecs = {}
    postfix = byte_order == '<' and '_le' or '_be'
    for k, v in template.items():
        codec = v['codec']
        try:
            " ".encode(codec)
        except LookupError:
            codecs[k] = None
            continue
        if v['width'] > 1:
            codec += postfix
        codecs[k] = codec
    return codecs.copy()


MDTYPES: dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, str]]] = {}
for _bytecode in '<>':
    _def = {'dtypes': convert_dtypes(mdtypes_template, _bytecode),
            'classes': convert_dtypes(mclass_dtypes_template, _bytecode),
            'codecs': _convert_codecs(codecs_template, _bytecode)}
    MDTYPES[_bytecode] = _def


class mat_struct:
    """Placeholder for holding read data from structs.

    We use instances of this class when the user passes False as a value to the
    ``struct_as_record`` parameter of the :func:`scipy.io.loadmat` function.
    """
    pass


class MatlabObject(np.ndarray):
    """Subclass of ndarray to signal this is a matlab object.

    This is a simple subclass of :class:`numpy.ndarray` meant to be used
    by :func:`scipy.io.loadmat` and should not be instantiated directly.

    Parameters
    ----------
    input_array : array-like
        The data to be stored in the array.
    classname : str, optional
        The MATLAB class name of the object.
    """

    def __new__(cls, input_array, classname=None):
        # Input array is an already formed ndarray instance
        # We first cast to be our class type
        obj = np.asarray(input_array).view(cls)
        # add the new attribute to the created instance
        obj.classname = classname
        # Finally, we must return the newly created object:
        return obj

    def __array_finalize__(self,obj):
        # reset the attribute from passed original object
        self.classname = getattr(obj, 'classname', None)
        # We do not need to return anything


class MatlabFunction(np.ndarray):
    """Subclass for a MATLAB function.

    This is a simple subclass of :class:`numpy.ndarray` meant to be used
    by :func:`scipy.io.loadmat` and should not be directly instantiated.

    Parameters
    ----------
    input_array : array-like
        The data to be stored in the array.
    """

    def __new__(cls, input_array):
        obj = np.asarray(input_array).view(cls)
        return obj


class MatlabOpaque(np.ndarray):
    """Subclass for a MATLAB opaque matrix.

    This is a simple subclass of :class:`numpy.ndarray` meant to be used
    by :func:`scipy.io.loadmat` and should not be directly instantiated.

    Parameters
    ----------
    input_array : array-like
        The data to be stored in the array.
    """

    def __new__(cls, input_array):
        obj = np.asarray(input_array).view(cls)
        return obj


OPAQUE_DTYPE = np.dtype(
    [('_TypeSystem', 'O'), ('_Class', 'O'), ('_ObjectMetadata', 'O')])

188	        self.cstream = _streams.make_stream(preader.mat_stream)

191	        self.chars_as_strings = preader.chars_as_strings
192	        self.squeeze_me = preader.squeeze_me
193	        # copy refs to dtypes into object pointer array. We only need the

196	            if isinstance(key, str):

206	        ''' Set stream of best type from file-like `fobj`

208	        Called from Python when initiating a variable read
209	        '''
210	        self.cstream = _streams.make_stream(fobj)
211	
212	    def read_tag(self):
213	        ''' Read tag mdtype and byte_count
214	
215	        Does necessary swapping and takes account of SDE formats.
216	
217	        See also ``read_full_tag`` method.
218	
219	        Returns
220	        -------
221	        mdtype : int
222	           matlab data type code
223	        byte_count : int
224	           number of bytes following that comprise the data
225	        tag_data : None or str
226	           Any data from the tag itself.  This is None for a full tag,
227	           and string length `byte_count` if this is a small data
228	           element.
229	        '''
230	        cdef cnp.uint32_t mdtype, byte_count
231	        cdef char tag_ptr[4]
232	        cdef int tag_res
233	        cdef object tag_data = None
234	        tag_res = self.cread_tag(&mdtype, &byte_count, tag_ptr)
235	        if tag_res == 2: # sde format
236	            tag_data = tag_ptr[:byte_count]
237	        return (mdtype, byte_count, tag_data)
238	
239	    cdef int cread_tag(self,
240	                     cnp.uint32_t *mdtype_ptr,
241	                     cnp.uint32_t *byte_count_ptr,
242	                     char *data_ptr) except -1:
243	        ''' Read tag mdtype and byte_count
244	
245	        Does necessary swapping and takes account of SDE formats
246	
247	        Data may be returned in data_ptr, if this was an SDE.
248	        data_ptr must point to a buffer of size >= 4 bytes.
249	
250	        Returns 1 for success, full format; 2 for success, SDE format; -1
251	        if error arises
252	        '''
253	        cdef cnp.uint16_t mdtype_sde, byte_count_sde
254	        cdef cnp.uint32_t mdtype
255	        cdef cnp.uint32_t* u4_ptr = <cnp.uint32_t*>data_ptr
256	        cdef cnp.uint32_t u4s[2]
257	        # First read 8 bytes.  The 8 bytes can be in one of two formats.
258	        # For the first - standard format - the 8 bytes are two uint32
259	        # values, of which the first is the integer code for the matlab
260	        # data type (*mdtype*), and the second is the number of bytes of
261	        # that data type that follow (*byte_count*).  Thus, if the
262	        # ``mdtype`` is 4 (miDOUBLE), and the ``byte_count`` is 12, then
263	        # there will follow 3 double values.  The alternative format is
264	        # "small data element". The first four bytes contain the
265	        # ``byte_count`` and the ``mdtype``, but as uint16.  The
266	        # arrangement of the ``byte_count`` and ``mdtype`` is a little
267	        # complex, see below. The following 4 bytes of the 8 bytes
268	        # contain the data.  For example, the ``mdtype`` might be 2
269	        # (miUINT8), and the byte count is 3, and the data is in a
270	        # string ``tag``, then the contained matrix is length 3, type
271	        # uint8, where values are ``tag[4], tag[5], tag[6]``.
272	        #
273	        # The following paragraph describes the extraction of ``mdtype``
274	        # and ``byte_count`` for the small data element format.  The
275	        # following is somewhat contrary to the matlab documentation,
276	        # but seems to be true of actual .mat files.
277	        #
278	        # If the *file* is big endian, then the first four bytes of the
279	        # tag are two big-endian uint16 values, first ``byte_count`` and
280	        # second ``mdtype``.  If the *file* is little-endian then the
281	        # first four bytes are two little-endian uint16 values, first
282	        # ``mdtype`` and second ``byte_count``.
283	        self.cstream.read_into(<void *>u4s, 8)
284	        if self.is_swapped:
285	            mdtype = byteswap_u4(u4s[0])
286	        else:
287	            mdtype = u4s[0]
288	        # The most significant two bytes of a U4 *mdtype* will always be
289	        # 0, if they are not, this must be SDE format
290	        byte_count_sde = mdtype >> 16
291	        if byte_count_sde: # small data element format
292	            mdtype_sde = mdtype & 0xffff
293	            if byte_count_sde > 4:
294	                raise ValueError('Error in SDE format data')
295	            u4_ptr[0] = u4s[1]
296	            mdtype_ptr[0] = mdtype_sde
297	            byte_count_ptr[0] = byte_count_sde
298	            return 2
299	        # regular element
300	        if self.is_swapped:
301	            byte_count_ptr[0] = byteswap_u4(u4s[1])
302	        else:
303	            byte_count_ptr[0] = u4s[1]
304	        mdtype_ptr[0] = mdtype
305	        u4_ptr[0] = 0
306	        return 1
307	
308	    cdef object read_element(self,
309	                             cnp.uint32_t *mdtype_ptr,
310	                             cnp.uint32_t *byte_count_ptr,
311	                             void **pp,
312	                             int copy=True):
313	        ''' Read data element into string buffer, return buffer
314	
315	        The element is the atom of the matlab file format.
316	
317	        Parameters
318	        ----------
319	        mdtype_ptr : uint32_t*
320	           pointer to uint32_t value to which we write the mdtype value
321	        byte_count_ptr : uint32_t*
322	           pointer to uint32_t value to which we write the byte count
323	        pp : void**
324	           pointer to void*. pp[0] will be set to point to the start of
325	           the returned string memory
326	        copy : int
327	           If not 0, do any copies required to allow memory to be freely
328	           altered without interfering with other objects.  Otherwise
329	           return string that should not be written to, therefore saving
330	           unnecessary copies
331	
332	        Return
333	        ------
334	        data : str
335	           Python string object containing read data
336	
337	        Notes
338	        -----
339	        See ``read_element_into`` for routine to read element into a
340	        pre-allocated block of memory.
341	        '''
342	        cdef cnp.uint32_t byte_count
343	        cdef char tag_data[4]
344	        cdef object data
345	        cdef int mod8
346	        cdef int tag_res = self.cread_tag(mdtype_ptr,
347	                                          byte_count_ptr,
348	                                          tag_data)
349	        byte_count = byte_count_ptr[0]
350	        if tag_res == 1: # full format
351	            data = self.cstream.read_string(
352	                byte_count,
353	                pp,
354	                copy)
355	            # Seek to next 64-bit boundary
356	            mod8 = byte_count % 8
357	            if mod8:
358	                self.cstream.seek(8 - mod8, 1)
359	        else: # SDE format, make safer home for data
360	            data = tag_data[:byte_count]
361	            pp[0] = <char *>data
362	        return data
363	
364	    cdef int read_element_into(self,
365	                               cnp.uint32_t *mdtype_ptr,
366	                               cnp.uint32_t *byte_count_ptr,
367	                               void *ptr,
368	                               cnp.uint32_t max_byte_count) except -1:
369	        ''' Read element into pre-allocated memory in `ptr`
370	
371	        Parameters
372	        ----------
373	        mdtype_ptr : uint32_t*
374	           pointer to uint32_t value to which we write the mdtype value
375	        byte_count_ptr : uint32_t*
376	           pointer to uint32_t value to which we write the byte count
377	        ptr : void*
378	           memory buffer into which to read
379	        max_byte_count : uint32_t
380	           size of the buffer pointed to by ptr
381	
382	        Returns
383	        -------
384	        void
385	
386	        Notes
387	        -----
388	        Compare ``read_element``.
389	        '''
390	        cdef:
391	           int mod8
392	        if max_byte_count < 4:
393	            raise ValueError('Unexpected amount of data to read (malformed input file?)')
394	        cdef int res = self.cread_tag(
395	            mdtype_ptr,
396	            byte_count_ptr,
397	            <char *>ptr)
398	        cdef cnp.uint32_t byte_count = byte_count_ptr[0]
399	        if res == 1: # full format
400	            if byte_count > max_byte_count:
401	                raise ValueError('Unexpected amount of data to read (malformed input file?)')
402	            res = self.cstream.read_into(ptr, byte_count)
403	            # Seek to next 64-bit boundary
404	            mod8 = byte_count % 8
405	            if mod8:
406	                self.cstream.seek(8 - mod8, 1)
407	        return 0
408	
409	    cpdef cnp.ndarray read_numeric(self, int copy=True, size_t nnz=-1):
410	        ''' Read numeric data element into ndarray
411	
412	        Reads element, then casts to ndarray.
413	
414	        The type of the array is usually given by the ``mdtype`` returned via
415	        ``read_element``.  Sparse logical arrays are an exception, where the
416	        type of the array may be ``np.bool_`` even if the ``mdtype`` claims the
417	        data is of float64 type.
418	
419	        Parameters
420	        ----------
421	        copy : bool, optional
422	            Whether to copy the array before returning.  If False, return array
423	            backed by bytes read from file.
424	        nnz : int, optional
425	            Number of non-zero values when reading numeric data from sparse
426	            matrices.  -1 if not reading sparse matrices, or to disable check
427	            for bytes data instead of declared data type (see Notes).
428	
429	        Returns
430	        -------
431	        arr : array
432	            Numeric array
433	
434	        Notes
435	        -----
436	        MATLAB apparently likes to store sparse logical matrix data as bytes
437	        instead of miDOUBLE (float64) data type, even though the data element
438	        still declares its type as miDOUBLE.  We can guess this has happened by
439	        looking for the length of the data compared to the expected number of
440	        elements, using the `nnz` input parameter.
441	        '''
442	        cdef cnp.uint32_t mdtype, byte_count
443	        cdef void *data_ptr
444	        cdef cnp.npy_intp el_count
445	        cdef cnp.ndarray el
446	        cdef object data = self.read_element(
447	            &mdtype, &byte_count, <void **>&data_ptr, copy)
448	        cdef cnp.dtype dt = <cnp.dtype>self.dtypes[mdtype]
449	        if dt.itemsize != 1 and nnz != -1 and byte_count == nnz:
450	            el_count = <cnp.npy_intp> nnz
451	            dt = BOOL_DTYPE
452	        else:
453	            el_count = byte_count // dt.itemsize
454	        cdef int flags = 0
455	        if copy:
456	            flags = cnp.NPY_ARRAY_WRITEABLE
457	        Py_INCREF(<object> dt)
458	        el = PyArray_NewFromDescr(&PyArray_Type,
459	                                   dt,
460	                                   1,
461	                                   &el_count,
462	                                   NULL,
463	                                   <void*>data_ptr,
464	                                   flags,
465	                                   <object>NULL)
466	        Py_INCREF(<object> data)
467	        PyArray_Set_BASE(el, data)
468	        return el
469	
470	    cdef inline object read_int8_string(self):
471	        ''' Read, return int8 type string
472	
473	        int8 type strings used for variable names, class names of
474	        objects, and field names of structs and objects.
475	
476	        Specializes ``read_element``
477	        '''
478	        cdef:
479	            cnp.uint32_t mdtype, byte_count, i
480	            void* ptr
481	            unsigned char* byte_ptr
482	            object data
483	        data = self.read_element(&mdtype, &byte_count, &ptr)
484	        if mdtype == miUTF8:  # Some badly-formed .mat files have utf8 here
485	            byte_ptr = <unsigned char*> ptr
486	            for i in range(byte_count):
487	                if byte_ptr[i] > 127:
488	                    raise ValueError('Non ascii int8 string')
489	        elif mdtype != miINT8:
490	            raise TypeError('Expecting miINT8 as data type')
491	        return data
492	
493	    cdef int read_into_int32s(self, cnp.int32_t *int32p, cnp.uint32_t max_byte_count) except -1:
494	        ''' Read int32 values into pre-allocated memory
495	
496	        Byteswap as necessary.  Specializes ``read_element_into``
497	
498	        Parameters
499	        ----------
500	        int32p : int32 pointer
501	        max_count : uint32_t
502	
503	        Returns
504	        -------
505	        n_ints : int
506	           Number of integers read
507	        '''
508	        cdef:
509	            cnp.uint32_t mdtype, byte_count, n_ints
510	            int i, check_ints=0
511	        self.read_element_into(&mdtype, &byte_count, <void *>int32p, max_byte_count)
512	        if mdtype == miUINT32:
513	            check_ints = 1
514	        elif mdtype != miINT32:
515	            raise TypeError('Expecting miINT32 as data type')
516	        n_ints = byte_count // 4
517	        if self.is_swapped:
518	            for i in range(n_ints):
519	                int32p[i] = byteswap_u4(int32p[i])
520	        if check_ints:
521	            for i in range(n_ints):
522	                if int32p[i] < 0:
523	                    raise ValueError('Expecting miINT32, got miUINT32 with '
524	                                     'negative values')
525	        return n_ints

527	    def read_full_tag(self):
528	        ''' Python method for reading full u4, u4 tag from stream
529

531	        -------

533	           matlab data type code
534	        byte_count : int32
535	           number of data bytes following
536	
537	        Notes
538	        -----
539	        Assumes tag is in fact full, that is, is not a small data
540	        element.  This means it can skip some checks and makes it
541	        slightly faster than ``read_tag``
542	        '''
543	        cdef cnp.uint32_t mdtype, byte_count
544	        self.cread_full_tag(&mdtype, &byte_count)
